title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. lazarei
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. lazarei is a subspecies of the rockrose plant, a type of flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. lazarei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antiviral properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, tinctures, or ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
